method: Live GHL production sweep via the backend REST API (PIT token, location Ghstz8eIsHWLeXek47dk): a 1,500-contact sample (of 15,967 total), a 500-opportunity sample, the full 126-field custom field schema, and the live custom-object registry. Cross-referenced against the 45-workflow live re-map from the 2026-07-22 pass to check which workflow, if any, writes each attribution field.
headline: Two disconnected attribution systems exist side by side. GHL's own native attributions[] array already auto-captures rich, accurate first/last-touch data (gclid, fbclid, fbc, fbp, gaClientId, UTMs, referrer, page URL) on 93% of contacts. The hand-built custom-field layer that reports and ad-platform uploads actually read from is separately broken: fbclid_value is 0% populated even on the 166 contacts (11.1% of the sample) where the native array has a real Facebook click ID, and gclid_value is 0% populated on all 69 contacts where the native array has a real Google click ID. The documented 'first-touch' field set (first_utm_source, first_gclid, first_fbclid, first_touch_at, first_landing_page) is 0% populated across the entire sample -- that layer was designed but never wired up. And 89.2% of Opportunities still carry $0 Monetary Value, so even where attribution exists, it has nothing to attach revenue to.

custom_fields.note: Zero custom fields of any kind exist on the Opportunity object today. Every attribution-related field GHL engineering built lives on Contact, which is overwritten on every new touch/visit -- the opposite of the 'Opportunity as attribution ledger' target design.

click_id_schema_gap.note: The native attributions[] array DOES capture gbraid automatically (confirmed live) even though no gbraid custom field exists to persist it -- so the raw signal exists but is dropped on the floor before it reaches any custom field, report, or ad-platform export.

mapping_failure_proof.title: Direct proof: the custom-field capture layer is disconnected from GHL's own native capture

rows.cohort: Contacts where native attributions[] has a REAL Facebook click ID (utmFbclid)
Contacts where native attributions[] has a REAL Google click ID (utmGclid)

mapping_failure_proof.interpretation: 100% of contacts with a verified click ID sitting in GHL's own attributions[] array have an EMPTY gclid_value/fbclid_value custom field. This is not a partial-capture problem -- whatever populates gclid_value/fbclid_value today runs entirely independently of GHL's native attribution capture (most likely a website-side write on form submit that parses the URL directly), and the two pipelines have zero verified overlap in this sample. No workflow in the 2026-07-22 live re-map of all 45 active workflows writes to either field -- the write path, wherever it is, is outside the audited workflow layer.

contact_to_opportunity_handoff.title: Attribution is dropped at the Contact to Opportunity handoff
contact_to_opportunity_handoff.interpretation: 95.4% of Opportunities DO carry a native attributions[] entry, but it is created at Opportunity-creation time by a workflow ('CRM Workflows' / 'Manual' medium) and does not carry forward the Contact's real first-touch marketing attribution. The Opportunity attribution entry and the Contact attribution entries are two separate records with no verified link -- the paid-media journey that produced the lead is not present on the deal that reports revenue against it.

overwrite_risk.title: Multi-touch overwrite risk cannot be assessed because the comparison fields are empty, not because there is no overwrite risk
overwrite_risk.why_zero_is_not_good_news: 0 divergence was measured between first_utm_source/utm_source and first_gclid/gclid_value on the 679 multi-touch contacts, but this is an artifact of first_utm_source and first_gclid being 0% populated account-wide (see population_rates) -- there is no live first-touch value to diverge FROM. The real overwrite exposure lives inside the native attributions[] array itself: 45.3% of the sample has 2+ attribution entries, and only the isFirst=true entry (91% of stamped entries) is reliably marked; there is no equivalent, reliably-populated isLast/current marker feeding the custom fields that reports actually read. Any report built off utm_source/gclid_value today is reading whichever touch last wrote the field, not a governed first-touch or last-touch value.

custom_objects.lead_source_object_status: Not found as a live custom object in the current registry. Earlier account notes describe an empty lead_source custom object; it either predates this object registry, was removed, or was renamed -- it is not the custom_objects.locations object, which is the per-clinic constants object built for Method 1 of the multi-location pattern (booking URL/phone/address), unrelated to attribution.
